questions.question: For a person, choose: The teacher {a1} helped me is kind

a1.verb_hint: Use who for people.

questions.question: For a thing, choose: The book {a1} I bought is useful

a1.verb_hint: Use which for things.

questions.question: After all, choose: This is all {a1} I know

a1.verb_hint: Use that after all in a defining clause.

questions.question: For possession, choose: The student {a1} bag is missing is upset

a1.verb_hint: Use whose before a noun for possession.

questions.question: Choose the subject pronoun: The woman {a1} called yesterday is my aunt

a1.verb_hint: The relative pronoun is the subject of called.

questions.question: Choose the object pronoun for a thing: The book {a1} I lost was new

a1.verb_hint: The relative pronoun refers to a thing as object.

questions.question: Do not use who for things: The book {a1} broke is old

a1.verb_hint: Use which, not who, for things.

questions.question: For a person after a comma, choose: My brother, {a1} lives nearby, is a doctor

a1.verb_hint: Use who for a person in a non-defining clause.

questions.question: Do not write who is for possession: The man {a1} car was stolen called us

a1.verb_hint: Use whose for possession.

questions.question: In formal style after a preposition, choose: The person to {a1} I spoke was polite

a1.verb_hint: Use whom after a preposition in formal style.

questions.question: After a superlative, choose: It is the best film {a1} I have seen

a1.verb_hint: Use that after a superlative.

questions.question: Do not add an extra subject: The man {a1} called is my boss

a1.verb_hint: Use who as the subject without another subject pronoun.
